title: Duplicate integrations when duplicating a board

posts.author: pszymanska (Patrycja)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
I’ve noticed that when I’m duplicating a board that already has some automations & integrations set up, only the automations get duplicated and integrations need to be done all over again.
Is there a way to duplicate those too?

Thanks for help!

posts.author: JohnMonte7 (John Monte)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m having the same issue as above. Really hoping integrations can be carried over if you’re duplicating a board.

posts.author: ErickE-monday.com (Erick E.)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello!

At the moment there is no way to duplicate integrations across boards and we’re only able to do so with limited automations. This is because integrations are mapped directly to the Board ID of the board to which they belong to and when a board is duplicated, the Board ID changes.

I will communicate this to our developers to see if supporting the ability to duplicate integrations across boards is something we may be able to implement in the future.

posts.author: JohnMonte7 (John Monte)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks. Even if before duplicating you had to toggl what integrations were carried over.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml: I think that the integrations and automations that can not be immediately activated could be copied, turned off and flagged as requiring edits before allowing them to be turned on. For us, this would make the use of templates significantly more useful.

posts.author: JessS (Jessica Somerville)
posts.cookedHtml: I would love this too - templates are kind of useless if they don’t duplicate integrations. We use templates to create several boards per day for each new project checklist and it’s a major pain that we can’t make use of the integrations feature. It’s pretty much the whole reason we signed up to Monday in the first place.

posts.author: rosovconsulting (Rosov Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, this is a good idea. Integrations that could be copied over will be, and those that can’t won’t (possibly with a notification about this).

Seems to be a similar request to Integrations and templates issues

posts.author: Marc1 (Marc Rosser)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi,

Is there any progress on this? This is truely a flaw if Integrations (especially Start + End = Timeline, seen as it should be a native automation and not a third-party feature) don’t follow templates you set up in a company.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
Integrations & automations are now copied along with the other components of a board when a board is duplicated or created using a template.

With the integrations, it is important to note that they are copied to the new board in a “ghost” state and must be re-added to the board.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml: This is great news @JCorrell . We have actually shied away from using some simple integrations with templates because they needed to be recreated each time. Awesome!

posts.author: ShawnBaden (shawn baden)
posts.cookedHtml: This is important for our workflows!

posts.author: RonTuretzky (Ron Turetzky)
posts.cookedHtml: But if I pack a board as a workspace template the integration recipe does carry over, just has to be reintialized with a connection, could work as a solution for the feature request here

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @JCorrell - is this working for you? I need some General Caster integrations to come across from a template and they are not (not even in ghost mode)? Have you had success with this?

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@mark.anley

In general, yes. What specifically are you trying to do that isn’t working? Is it just a normal template with attached apps integrations?

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @JCorrell - yes thats correct. I have a single template board (open permissions) and a single General Caster integration included. When I create a new board based off this template (manually or via automation) it doesnt include the integration.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@mark.anley

It appears that when creating a board from a template. Built-in integrations are copying into “ghost” status. While integrations from apps are not. I don’t know if this would apply to workspace templates.

I also do not know if this is a new “feature” or has always been this way. I can tell you that there have been some VERY recent (like in the last few days) changes in the area of integrations with apps. We are currently waiting on monday to get back to us with additional information.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks @JCorrell - I just tested the same thing myself and noted the same result which is unfortunate. Let me know if you see/hear anything else based on the recent changes…
Appreciate the help as always.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@mark.anley

My pleasure! I’ll let you know if/when I hear anything more.

posts.author: greg-hrc (Greg Collette)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have a seemingly related request - it would be super helpful if we could templatize an integration recipe like we can with automation recipes.

For example, I created a webhook integration with Zapier for removing the “(copy)” from duplicated items’ names. I’d like to make it available to the rest of our company to add to their boards without having to know the webhook URL or how to set it up.

posts.author: jailynnhoops (Jai-Lynn Hoops)
posts.cookedHtml: @JCorrell or @mark.anley have either of you seen any upcoming edits for this? I have a webhook integration that is using Make and it is not copying over. Would really love to see this at least be a ghost option!

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@jailynnhoops

Are you duplicating a board or creating a new one using a template?

posts.author: jailynnhoops (Jai-Lynn Hoops)
posts.cookedHtml: @JCorrell We have done both methods.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@jailynnhoops

It appears that you are not the only one having an issue with this at the moment.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
I know that monday is changing the duplication process. In mid-September monday made a change where they all of a sudden copied the webwooks over to the duplicated board. Because the duplication process also call the subscribe endpoint (in case of custom triggers) and this endpoint creates the webhooks itself you ended up with two webhooks for each type. I worked intensively with support and it was finally solved this weekend. It looks like you are experiencing a side-effect from this fix.

I would contact appsupport@monday.com with this one.

posts.author: ofuks001 (Omara Fuks)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for the info @basdebruin Do you know if they will enable it again? Is there any other way to keep webhooks activate when duplicating boards? Thanks for your help!

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @ofuks001

I think the only one that can answer that question is monday.com

posts.author: jailynnhoops (Jai-Lynn Hoops)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone!

Follow up information on this issue. I currently have seen that Integrations that I have created (that are for the item and subitem level) are copying over well! It keeps the recipes when I duplicate or use the board as a template. However, when I have a colleague (who is not the integration creator) duplicate or use the template, it doesn’t copy over properly on the item or subitem level.

This just leaves a HUGE bottleneck for me to have to create each board.

posts.author: JatSaber (JimT)
posts.cookedHtml:
Me too. Integrations duplicate across for me, but not others who duplicate the master board that I created.

It seemed like this did work last year while we were Beta testing our set up. We went live on Jan 1, and doesn’t seem to work now. I’m not 100% sure we checked this particular issue, so don’t quote me on that.

posts.author: cshebesta (Christian Shebesta)
posts.cookedHtml:
All automations transfer over for us, but gmail integrations are still not supported (just confirmed with support). Transferring over these integrations would transform our process. It is simply too time intensive to recreate them for each project we create.

This has been tested with 3 methods:

1.) Creating a Workspace template
2.) Using a Board template
3.) Duplicating the board with the integrations

posts.author: Trivia (Trivia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Jim,

I just created a template last week with several automations and none of them transferred when the board was duplicated. Did you have to use an integration for yours to copy?

posts.cookedHtml:

posts.author: Nadi (Andreas)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Hugo,

Could you please show me how to configure this using Make?

Much appreciated
